diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A known pre-existing wart establishes the benign baseline lesion that was then traumatised.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute mechanical shear trauma with partial avulsion of the keratotic cap and minor bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A discrete traumatic event on a known lesion explains the acute pain crisis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial cellulitis / deep soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An open avulsed surface is a portal for infection and must be safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe localised pain with complete inability to weight-bear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exposed nerve endings under an avulsed plantar cap produce disproportionate pain; drives the same-day in-person need rather than the tier of the underlying benign lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No systemic symptoms (denies fever, chills, feeling unwell)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial cellulitis / deep soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of systemic features makes current established infection unlikely, but does not exclude early or evolving infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spreading erythema, purulent discharge or fluctuance on examination (1 cm lesion, well-localised)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial cellulitis / deep soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician examination finding — absence of spreading signs supports a benign uncomplicated course; the AI Doctor can only partially proxy this from a patient photo and must defer to in-person review.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermoscopic thrombosed capillaries with no visible foreign material and no atypical (irregular border / varied pigmentation / rapid growth) features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thrombosed capillaries are characteristic of a viral wart and argue against a retained foreign body and against malignancy — a sealed dermoscopic finding requiring in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: retained foreign body in the sole
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No visible foreign material on dermoscopy; nonetheless full exclusion needs hands-on examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs within normal limits (BP 135/85, HR 92, Temp 36.9, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: secondary bacterial cellulitis / deep soft-tissue infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal observations argue against systemic sepsis — sealed clinician measurement not obtainable via chat without a device; the AI Doctor reasons from the patient's subjective report of feeling well.
